Dabhol power project in India, the company said on Monday, ending a four-year 
struggle to keep the project alive. 
The company will sell its 50 percent stake in the $2.9 billion project, which 
is India's largest single foreign investment, to the Maharashtra State Electricity 
Board (MSEB), Enron said in a statement. 
The sale will help MSEB repay a $1.2 billion loan it took from the U.S. energy group 
in March, the statement said.

Enron said.

11/02/2001
Reuters English News Service
(C) Reuters Limited 2001.

NEW DELHI, Nov 2 (Reuters) - Enron Corp's Dabhol Power Co Ltd said on Monday it had agreed to pay $1.1 billion to end a power crisis in India. 
The payment, the largest ever by a foreign company to resolve a dispute in India, was part of a $2.2 billion package of loans and guarantees that the U.S. energy group had offered to help the financially ailing state-run power utility, the Press Trust of India news agency reported. 
The payment was the first by a foreign company to resolve a dispute between the state-run Maharashtra State Electricity Board (MSEB) and Enron, the report said.

Enron's Dabhol Power Project: India's State Bank To Invest $1.5 Bln

10/27/2001
Reuters English News Service
(C) Reuters Limited 2001.

NEW DELHI, Oct 27 (Reuters) - India's State Bank of India will invest $1.5 billion in the $2.5 billion Dabhol Power Co., the country's biggest foreign direct investment, the bank said on Monday. 
The bank, the country's largest lender, will also take a controlling stake in the project, the largest foreign direct investment in India, the statement said. 
The project, which is India's biggest foreign direct investment, is owned by U.S.-based Enron Corp. and is India's largest single foreign investment.
